subject: [PATCH v2 15/28] nfsd: use RCU to protect fi_deleg_file

2026-04-16.patch_summary: This patch uses RCU (Read-Copy-Update) to protect the fi_deleg_file pointer in the nfsd kernel module. The issue it solves is a race condition where concurrent accesses from workqueue callbacks can dereference a NULL pointer if they occur during delegation teardown. To fix this, the patch annotates fi_deleg_file with __rcu and converts all accessors to use RCU primitives such as rcu_assign_pointer() and rcu_dereference_protected(). This prepares for a subsequent patch that will safely acquire a reference from the CB_NOTIFY callback path without holding the fi_lock. The approach taken is to use RCU to ensure that the fi_deleg_file pointer remains valid even when it is being updated or deleted, thus preventing NULL pointer dereferences.
